---
schema: verdi.decisionconflict/v1
covers: c9c730fbad7b8b1f0b383fb4901cd2965b52d32a
findings:
  - { id: judged-bounded-walk-vs-verbatim-fold-rule, kind: judged, text: "Story dc-1 bounds sync's fetch-time ancestor walk (a per-candidate forge-call cost argument, exact bound deferred to build time), but parent spec/closure-ergonomics dc-3 requires the fold's ancestor rule 'applied verbatim at fetch time' and declares that wherever the two surfaces would disagree the fold's rule is authoritative and sync conforms. The fold's LoadRecordsWithSources filter is unbounded over what exists on disk, so any bound on the fetch walk creates commits that are genuine ancestors under the fold's rule yet never probed by sync — exactly a surface disagreement the parent decision forbids, and sync is the side that deviates. The story discloses the bound as a build-time judgment but declares no supersedes/exempts edge against the parent dc-3, leaving the D6-32 asymmetry it claims to close partially reopened in the sync-stricter direction. (confidence 0.65)", target_ref: spec/closure-ergonomics }
sweep_provenance: { adr_corpus_digest: sha256:37517e5f3dc66819f61f5a7bb8ace1921282415f10551d2defa5c3eb0985b570, decisions_scanned: [spec/closure-ergonomics#dc-1, spec/closure-ergonomics#dc-2, spec/closure-ergonomics#dc-3, spec/closure-ergonomics#dc-4, spec/closure-ergonomics#dc-5, spec/sync-local-flow#dc-1, spec/sync-local-flow#dc-2, spec/sync-local-flow#dc-3] }

integrity: sha256:ba2895c21e1b5c32a90ab054bc63470cceae7cdf1e91af5915916beb9bb9e5d1
judge_integrity: { stdin_b64: , raw_result: "{\"findings\":[{\"id\":\"bounded-walk-vs-verbatim-fold-rule\",\"text\":\"Story dc-1 bounds sync's fetch-time ancestor walk (a per-candidate forge-call cost argument, exact bound deferred to build time), but parent spec/closure-ergonomics dc-3 requires the fold's ancestor rule 'applied verbatim at fetch time' and declares that wherever the two surfaces would disagree the fold's rule is authoritative and sync conforms. The fold's LoadRecordsWithSources filter is unbounded over what exists on disk, so any bound on the fetch walk creates commits that are genuine ancestors under the fold's rule yet never probed by sync — exactly a surface disagreement the parent decision forbids, and sync is the side that deviates. The story discloses the bound as a build-time judgment but declares no supersedes/exempts edge against the parent dc-3, leaving the D6-32 asymmetry it claims to close partially reopened in the sync-stricter direction.\",\"confidence\":0.65,\"target\":\"spec/closure-ergonomics\"}]}" }

# Decision-conflict report

## Computed (declared-edge completeness)

(none)

## Judged (undeclared-conflict sweep)

- **judged-bounded-walk-vs-verbatim-fold-rule** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: Story dc-1 bounds sync's fetch-time ancestor walk (a per-candidate forge-call cost argument, exact bound deferred to build time), but parent spec/closure-ergonomics dc-3 requires the fold's ancestor rule 'applied verbatim at fetch time' and declares that wherever the two surfaces would disagree the fold's rule is authoritative and sync conforms. The fold's LoadRecordsWithSources filter is unbounded over what exists on disk, so any bound on the fetch walk creates commits that are genuine ancestors under the fold's rule yet never probed by sync — exactly a surface disagreement the parent decision forbids, and sync is the side that deviates. The story discloses the bound as a build-time judgment but declares no supersedes/exempts edge against the parent dc-3, leaving the D6-32 asymmetry it claims to close partially reopened in the sync-stricter direction. (confidence 0.65)
